message:
fix(cli): bound response body reads with io.LimitReader

Two io.ReadAll(resp.Body) sites lacked any upper bound on the number
of bytes they'd pull from a remote server.  A hostile or broken
endpoint could feed an unbounded stream and OOM the CLI.

internal/cli/ensemble_presets.go
- readEnsembleImportSource(): wrap resp.Body in
  io.LimitReader(..., 100*1024*1024) before io.ReadAll.  100MB is
  comfortably above any legitimate ensemble export payload (in
  practice these are kilobytes) while still giving headroom for
  user-chosen bundles that embed assets.  If a server tries to feed
  more than 100MB we silently truncate and the downstream parser
  will reject the partial file instead of the process being killed
  by the OOM killer.

internal/cli/upgrade.go
- fetchLatestRelease(): on non-200 responses we read the body just
  to surface an error message.  Bound that read at 1*1024*1024 via
  io.LimitReader.  The GitHub API error body is always tiny; any
  response that would be >1MB here is either a misconfigured proxy
  or an attacker, and we don't need the remainder to produce a
  useful error message.

No tests added — these are one-line defensive wraps, and the
existing call sites are exercised by the upgrade/ensemble import
integration paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
